title: agent_presets in the config layer, rejected at load when malformed
action: Add an agent_presets setting to the gateway config: a list of { label, argv } entries. The mobile app will render one action row per preset; this cell is where they come from. FOLLOW allowed_roots, the only existing list-valued setting, at every step -- it already demonstrates the whole path. THE SEVEN TOUCH SITES (missing one is the ordinary way a config field ends up half-supported; the first five are this cell, site 6 is the next cell, site 7 is a later slice): (1) the Config struct, src/config/mod.rs:27-41. (2) RawConfig plus its serde default, src/config/mod.rs:90-106 -- use #[serde(default)] so the field is OPTIONAL and defaults to an empty list (CONTEXT.md P2). A gateway with no presets is normal and must boot fine. This is also why you must NOT touch either of the two hand-written default-config JSON literals at src/config/mod.rs:759-764 and src/doctor/checks.rs:790-793. (3) validation and construction in Config::load_str, src/config/mod.rs:164-211 -- collect failures into the existing errors vec the same way the other fields do, so multiple bad presets are all reported at once rather than one per run. (4) THE CONFIG_FIELDS ALLOWLIST, src/config/write.rs:16-25. READ THIS TWICE: repair_fields (src/config/write.rs:159-190) rebuilds the config document from CONFIG_FIELDS alone, so a field present in the struct but absent from that list is SILENTLY DELETED from the operator's config on every doctor-driven save. That is the single most expensive mistake available in this cell and it will not show up in any test you are likely to write by accident -- write one that would catch it. (5) the per-field validator in invalid_field_names, src/config/write.rs:192-218, alongside the existing *_ok functions. VALIDITY RULES (CONTEXT.md P3, P4): a preset is invalid when its label is empty, its argv is empty, or its first argv element is empty; and labels must be unique across the list, because two rows reading the same word in the phone sheet and doing different things is a defect the operator cannot diagnose from the phone. An invalid preset is a CONFIG-LOAD FAILURE, never a preset that loads and renders disabled (CONTEXT.md, the decided open question) -- this matches the file's existing posture, where an unknown key, an empty allowed_roots and a token-in-config all refuse rather than degrade. Name the offending entry by index AND label in the error so an operator can find it in a file they may have several of. Add a ConfigError variant for it rather than reusing Parse. WHAT NOT TO DO: do not validate that the argv names a program that exists -- the gateway does not know the desktop's PATH, herdr reports a spawn failure at start time, and pretending to check would be theatre (CONTEXT.md P6). Do not add editing UI -- that is cell agent-presets-config-2. TEST NAMING IS PART OF THE CONTRACT: prefix every test you add with presets_ (for example presets_absent_defaults_to_empty_list, presets_empty_argv_fails_load, presets_duplicate_labels_fail_load, presets_error_names_the_entry, presets_survive_a_doctor_repair_write). The verify requires at least 6 passing presets_ tests, so it fails on an untouched tree.

truths: A config with no agent_presets key loads fine and yields an empty list
A preset with an empty argv fails config load
A preset with an empty first argv element fails config load
A preset with an empty label fails config load
Two presets sharing a label fail config load
The load error names the offending entry by index and by label
Several bad presets are all reported from one load, not one per run
A valid preset list survives a doctor-driven field repair write instead of being silently dropped
Every pre-existing config test stays green
artifacts: agent_presets on Config and RawConfig with an empty-list default
Validation in Config::load_str feeding the existing multi-error collection
A ConfigError variant naming a bad preset by index and label
agent_presets in CONFIG_FIELDS and a matching validator in invalid_field_names
A test that would fail if agent_presets were left out of CONFIG_FIELDS
At least 6 tests prefixed presets_, all passing
key_links: src/config/mod.rs:1-14 -- the three refusals this file already makes, and the posture a preset must match
src/config/write.rs:159-190 -- repair_fields rebuilding the document from CONFIG_FIELDS only
src/config/write.rs:233-246 -- allowed_roots_ok, the validator shape to mirror
docs/history/agent-presets-config/CONTEXT.md -- P1 through P6 and the decided open question
prohibitions: Do not make agent_presets required -- absent means an empty list
Do not touch either hand-written default-config JSON literal
Do not let an invalid preset load as a disabled entry
Do not reuse ConfigError::Parse for a bad preset
Do not check whether the argv's program exists on disk
Do not add fields to a preset beyond label and argv
Do not add or change any doctor editing UI -- next cell
Do not weaken deny_unknown_fields or any existing validation to make room

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- presets_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([6-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: agent_presets landed in the config layer: on Config and RawConfig with an empty default, validated at load with a dedicated error naming a bad entry by index and label, registered in CONFIG_FIELDS with a validator so a doctor repair cannot silently drop it. The doctor menu tests now derive the stop index from CONFIG_FIELDS.len() instead of hardcoding it. 12 presets_ tests.
trace.verify_output: ORCHESTRATOR'S OWN FRESH RUN. cargo test --quiet: 213 passed, 0 failed (201 before). fmt: silent. clippy -D warnings: silent. presets_-only: 12 passed, 0 failed, 201 filtered out. Guard grep exit 0.
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- presets_ | grep -qE 'test result ok 6 or more passed'
